sourceMovie.title: Neighbors
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Neighbors for the same reason it broke through in 2014: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Nicholas Stoller keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: This Is the End
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Neighbors clicked for you, This Is the End (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Neighbors?
faq.answer: Try This Is the End, Game Night, Meet the Robinsons—each shares DNA with Neighbors while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Neighbors?
faq.answer: Neighbors (2014) is best described as Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Neighbors part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Neighbors Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
